items.title: While the world watches Iran, bombs are still dropping in Gaza

children.content: Palestinians in Gaza fear they are being forgotten as the Iran war drags on, with Israeli strikes continuing despite a ceasefire and the humanitarian situation still dire.

items.title: US alliance indispensable as Marles looks to build more self-sufficient ADF

image.altText: Richard Marles speaks at the National Press Club.

children.content: Defence Minister Richard Marles has declared the United States alliance will remain indispensable for Australian national security even while Australia looks to build a more self-reliant defence force.

items.title: Israel takes out key bridge in Lebanon, Hegseth threatens Iranian infrastructure

children.content: An Israeli strike has severed the last bridge linking southern Lebanon to the rest of the country ahead of mooted talks between Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Lebanese President Joseph Aoun.

image.altText: Members of the media work at the site after an Israeli strike severed a bridge in Lebanon.

items.title: Australian dollar surges to four-year high as traders see end to Iran war

children.content: Analysts say the mood on global financial markets has improved considerably over the past few days.

image.altText: Gold dollar coins on bank notes.

items.title: 100m litres of diesel secured under new powers, PM confirms

children.content: Anthony Albanese says that new powers allowing the federal government to underwrite the delivery of additional fuel cargoes have been used to purchase 100 million litres of diesel.

articles.title: Why Australia turned to tiny nation to secure critical fertiliser supplies

children.content: The Middle East conflict has disrupted the flow of Australia's imports of the fertiliser urea. Facing the potential threat of reduced crop yields, Anthony Albanese looked to Australia's neighbours.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese in a grey and green jacket and white hard hat, smiling while holding up two jars of blue and brown urea.

gallery.altText: Two men sit at desk in front of a row of flags.

thumbnail.altText: Two men sit at desk in front of a row of flags.

items.title: The cause of the Viva Energy oil refinery fire under investigation. Here's what we know

children.content: Firefighters have managed to extinguish a blaze at one of Australia's major oil refineries. Here's what we know so far about the cause of the fire — and the possible impact on fuel prices.

image.altText: fire and smoke pictured above the Geelong refinery in the evening

items.title: Hanson wishes Coalition good luck, government focuses on fuel

children.content: This week in Canberra, Pauline Hanson learned imitation is the sincerest form of flattery, the defence budget was on the agenda and a fire threw the fuel crisis into sharp relief.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson

items.title: Pope blasts 'tyrants' spending big on war following public spat with Trump

children.content: The US-born pope has praised peace movements and peace makers during his visit to the African nation of Cameroon as the rift with US President Donald Trump intensifies.

image.altText: A man in white robes is surrounded by people and cameras as he waves

items.title: Russia launches deadliest attack on Ukraine this year, killing 17

children.content: Russian forces have attacked the Ukrainian capital Kyiv and other cities, killing
people, as the defence leaders of more than 50 countries say they are aiming to send more aid to Ukraine.

image.altText: Two firefighters work as huge orange flames and black smoke fill the night sky, at a recycling centre.

items.title: Prince Harry tells summit he's 'felt lost, betrayed, or completely powerless’

children.content: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex are back together in Melbourne after spending the second day of their Australian tour apart.

items.title: Trbojevic injures hamstring as Manly thrash Cowboys

children.content: The Sea Eagles have smashed North Queensland but the win has come at a heavy cost following a hamstring injury to star fullback Tom Trbojevic.

image.altText: Tom Trbojevic looks despondent after suffering an injury

items.title: Daicos inspires Magpies to win as Blues youngster misses kick after siren

children.content: Nick Daicos stars in the final quarter to inspire Collingwood to a win as Carlton youngster Talor Byrne misses a kick after the siren to tie the scores.

items.title: 'No-one's committed murder here': Port CEO urges change to tribunal process

children.content: Port Adelaide's chief executive has slammed the AFL Tribunal process, labelling it "too legalistic" and "too adversarial".

image.altText: A man is a suit is frowning as he looks toward his left side.

items.title: NSW premier told to resign after protest laws struck down by top court

children.content: The laws, rushed through parliament after the Bondi Beach terrorist attack, have been found to be unconstitutional by the state's highest court.

image.altText: A large group of police and riot squad officers push back a crowd of protesters in the Sydney CBD.

items.title: Report finds Darwin Harbour biodiversity rivals that of Ningaloo Reef

children.content: New research has revealed Darwin Harbour's biodiversity, including its wide range of rare species, rivals that of the World Heritage-protected Ningaloo Reef.

items.title: Is LIV Golf dead? Not yet, but the vultures are circling

children.content: Rumours of LIV Golf's immediate demise look wide of the mark, but the smoke is only growing thicker as denials from the league's CEO help paint a bleak picture.

items.title: New data reveals age group spending up big

children.content: Australians spent more in March as the outbreak of the Iran war drove up petrol prices. Older Australians increased their spending at the fastest pace, CBA data shows.

image.altText: A close-up of $100 notes fanning out from a wallet.

items.title: Farmers 'running on empty' welcome arrival of diesel

children.content: A massive crude oil tanker laden with diesel has docked in the Port of Newcastle to replenish regional New South Wales supplies, as the Middle East unrest continues.

image.altText: A bulk tanker on the water

items.title: Smoky Sydney as 5,000 hectares burned in hazard reduction blitz

children.content: Smoky conditions across Greater Sydney are tipped to ease tomorrow as the wind picks up, after firefighters had a productive week burning hazards around Sydney's fringes.

image.altText: a smoke column rises in a green forest seen from far away

items.title: Man, 53, charged over seizure of more than 350 cannabis plants

children.content: A 53-year-old Kalgoorlie man is released on bail after the seizure of more than 350 cannabis plants from a property WA Police allege had been converted for the purpose of growing hydroponic cannabis.

image.altText: Photos of a hydronic cannabis operation supplied by police.

items.title: Queensland massage therapist faces 18 sexual assault charges

children.content: A Queensland massage therapist arrested in February over the alleged sexual assault of four clients is facing 11 fresh charges after another four female clients came forward.

image.altText: A man with a receding hairline and rough stubble smiles outdoors.

items.title: 'Snacks' ordered for alleged hit men's escape after underworld killing, court hears

children.content: Victoria's Supreme Court is told underworld figure Gavin Preston was cautious about his surroundings and "never" sat outside a cafe he regularly attended — other than the day he was shot dead.

image.altText: Crime figure Gavin Preston looks unsmilingly into the camera as he poses for a photo.

items.title: CFMEU leaders had 'the ear' of former state government, inquiry hears

children.content: Australia's unemployment rate remained at 4.3 per cent in March, but economists say cracks could start appearing in coming months.

items.title: Pandemic, 'ice epidemic' worsened Qld child safety pressures, ex-minister says
